reviews.review_description: I love horror but I’m always a scaredy-cat. I was able to finish this, even though I was terrified! I loved that it was long too! The creators did an awesome job with the story and making it immersive. I felt I was in there. I saw someone post a walk through of a DLC for this. How can I buy a DLC for it?

reviews.review_title: Great puzzle game, average horror game
reviews.review_description: It’s a great puzzle game but not very scary, horror fans will be bored as hell if scare factor is what you’re looking for. The story is great, I like the environments and visuals the gameplay is just slow, story driven puzzle gameplay all the way through.

Note: This post contains some game spoilers.

I enjoyed playing this game in VR, despite the fact that there are some game bugs which prevent the player from unlocking certain endings, in particular the self-portrait ending. In chapter 5 for example, there is a door which can be unbolted by using a crank, however the crank doesn't work, forcing the player to instead pull a chain to unbolt the door. Following a walk through for the self-portrait ending, I think not being able to unbolt the door with the crank prevents the self-portrait ending.
I love this game.  The jump scares are awesome!. I need part 2 and 3 ASAP!. I will be waiting.

reviews.review_title: Glitchy, simple, Dark, not that scary
reviews.review_description: I want to start off by saying; I am biased because my game glitches out, and i wasn't able to continue where I left off at... but that could just be me. Aside from that its a simple game where you walk through doors experiencing the simple yet psychotic life of a (schizophrenic?) Painter, who is trying to encapsulate his emotions in his paintings but neglecting his family in the pursuit of doing so. I understand it is a rather old game. But there's not a lot of good (not demonic) horror games on VR so this was kinda nice. Other than that I glitched out after witnessing a burning piano and as I went through a door (or attempted to) I got teleported out of bounds... all I could hear was a baby crying and a door squeaking (repetitively). If I could attach an image I would. Just don't buy the game, or buy it and return it before the 2 hour window where you can't refund it any more like I tried to do but failed. Hopefully this let's you know enough about it to understand what it is. It is also really dark and you can't see anything unless your contrast is at 0

reviews.review_title: I Don’t Know How Much I Can Take!
reviews.review_description: This Game Is Such A Struggle To Play Just Because Of How Scary It Is. Probably One Of The Best Psychological￼ Horrors Ever!

I've replayed this game all the way through several times, so obviously I’ve enjoyed it.  If I have one primary complaint, it is that some of the keys and collectibles are WAY TOO HARD TO FIND.  I still haven’t found all of them despite several play-throughs specifically dedicated to finding the rest.  The wall safes are probably the worst part of the game; combination safes just aren’t a good idea for VR.  The jump scares are good, but only work as well as they do because of the overall atmosphere.  There are some technical issues with the game that you’ll learn to avoid by trial and error.   There are areas that will glitch and loop and will require a restart in order to continue.  Once back in the game, you need to do things differently than before, or you will get stuck in the same loop.  Sometimes looking up the chapter you’re in online can help if you keep getting stuck.  If you like mystery and horror, I highly recommend this game, just be aware of the technical issues you may encounter.

I played this game on my pc with me meta quest 3 . Lots of scary fun . Definitely worth a play 👻😇
Love the horror painting / ink them to it all .
it's so dark I can't see where I am or where I'm going plus it glitches out repeatedly.

This game reminded me of the film 1408 with John Cusack fantastic story brilliant effects if you want to be scared then this is the game! I’m hoping there’s more to come from layers of fear on the VR 🤞🏻. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ from me and well done to the creators of this game amazingly talented people you need to make more!

Who the hell is writing all of the reviews full of praise?  I’m a horror lightweight normally.  I would, frankly, hide behind the sofa at the slightly louder elements of Tom & Jerry.  But,this effort, even for the 40% off sale price, is not worth it. Never have I been so bored whilst playing a VR game. 

My overall impressions of this game were, squinting at brown, and lots of it.  Oh yes, and grey.   Inadequate lighting springs to mind now also.  Somebody mentioned sound effects in one of the reviews?  Very clunky. (maybe an alarm clock now and again would have been a good thing?) Opening and closing lots of drawers  and doors, picking up bottles. (we get the picture, guy was alcohol dependent) Crouching.  Endlessly roaming around corridores with next to nothing happening.  A ball rolled out of a bedroom at one point (Eek!). Did I mention brown? To cap it all off, motion sickness! 

Serious question now:  What is the point of this game?  Is there a goal? 

To the developers, I can only leave you with the advice, garnered from the scribblings on my note books, from my old R.E. teacher, “must try harder”.

First 10 minutes of the game I go to walk upstairs and my player falls through. I am now stuck in the stairs without a way to get up. I’ve restarted the game several times but every time I try to go upstairs my player falls through.
Game is terrible

Bruh at first I didn’t really know what to think, then the jump scares started. Good story line and I love the mystery and unknown feeling. I almost had a heart attack like 6 times 😂 definitely worth it!

I am a massive fan of anything horror and after picking up an Oculus quest 2 a little over a month ago I have been going through most of the horror games the quest has to offer. When I saw that Layers of Fear was offered in the quest store I got excited and couldn't wait to play it.

I fell in love with the game when I first played it a few years ago on my Xbox one and have played it many more times since then. From the gripping story of a painter descending further into madness and revealing the tragic backstory through notes scattered throughout the ever shifting mansion to the dark and eerie atmosphere of the Victorian mansion itself that you explore, watching it change with the protagonist's declining sanity.

This port over to the quest is a pretty good adaptation of the original game to VR. The controls of the game are easy to understand and the tracking for your hands and head in game is fantastic. The port also keeps intact the original game's gripping story and amazing atmospheric music with no changes from the original that I was able to notice.

Even with both the great story and music remaining intact, this port does have a few downsides. One of the biggest downsides that I have with the VR version of the game is that the visuals from the original release back in 2016 have taken a pretty big hit. A big part of the original game that sold the atmosphere was the lighting. You could walk into a dimly lit room and see that there were small candles or other light fixtures lighting sections of the room while cloaking other parts into shadow. In the quest version much of that difference in lighting is straight up missing in this most with rooms still having a few light sources but the darkness has just been brightened up over all with the darker sections seeming not as dark anymore. Another big hit that the game took was to both the colors of the environment and the textures. The original game had many objects strewn throughout the mansion's floors and tables such as books, glasses, statues, dolls, furniture, etc. In the quest version the amount of these objects has decreased dramatically and the objects that do remain seem appear more blocky and with lower quality textures put on them. The color palette of the rooms seem to be more dull too with much of the environment looking more grey or brown and washed out compared to the original's more vibrant colors that were more contrasted with the darker areas in a room. This drab color palette of the quest version seemed to blend the rooms together more and make it harder to distinguish one room from another.  I understand that the quest 2 does not have the horsepower that a console or PC has but there have been much better looking horror games on the quest 2 and with the downgrade in visuals it really put a damper on the atmosphere for me. Lastly, when playing through the game I ran into a few bugs that I did not experience in the original game. One example is I walked into a room and there was a bright pink texture behind one of the windows instead of it's usual dark texture like other windows have. Another bug that I experienced was when trying to solve a puzzle with a phone the interact icon for the dial did not show up so the game would not register the numbers I dialed into the phone and I could not advance in the game and had to restart from the last checkpoint. The bugs that I experienced weren't bad but just a little annoying and immersion breaking.

Overall I think the game is absolutely still worth buying if you want to experience this game in VR and the quest 2 version is the only way that you can play the game or you don't care about the visual downgrades. However, in my opinion I would recommend playing this game on another PC or console if you have the option or if you wanted to play this in VR to play the VR version for the PC. Hope everyone has fun and enjoy your descent into madness.

This game is great, and really scary. Maybe too scary. I am scared to play anymore of this game because the previous section of the game scared the HELL out of me, and made me afraid of stuff like a slightly opened door in my house. So if you're easily scared, don't play this game (or do, and suffer) because it's terrifying.
Don't recommend

Wow just played about an hour so far and this is an extremely tense game. I would say the best way to describe it is Transference meets RE7,  a great compliment. The graphics are fantastic 9/10 most of the items are interactive and the environments have a great amount of detail. Especially when the environments start changing and playing tricks on you. The audio is a 10/10 very atmospheric and great positional sound, the sound of lightning sounds just like the real thing and I like how the clock in the room gets louder as you get closer. I highly recommend a nice set of headphones as you really get sucked into the game. Horror games are my thing and this and fnaf in the same month is awesome. It’s amazing getting big PC level games on the quest and can’t wait to see how this story plays out.

reviews.review_title: The most amazing awesome horror game!!!!
reviews.review_description: This game is so fun and amazing!!! Layers of fear is an amazing game the puzzles are fun and awesome but beware of the scares they did get me and I’m going to say that this is the best horror game out there on VR! The mind twisting stories make the game even more fun as you uncover the truth. Overall experience is awesome love the graphics and detail and also love the storyline. The storyline in this game is far more interesting and I say it’s for the right price and the best horror game out there!!!!

Just bought the quest 2 
Because I wanted to feel terror , I  wanted to be In the horror movies  and see how i'd manage the stress .
Layers of fear is beautifull , I like watching the paintings (nice work from the artists ) .
The jump-scares are really epic ,well-timed  and obviously the atmosphere is rich with tension . I would absolutely reccomend this game .
Played last night and only lasted 5 minutes  hahaha
